goal: agents copy pass: keep  namespace, retag user-facing strings as 'agent guidance'

summary.title: agents copy pass: retag user-facing strings as 'agent guidance' (namespace stays)
summary.what: Rewrite user-visible copy across the `mainline agents` family so it consistently says 'agent guidance' instead of the internal 'managed block' / 'Mainline template' vocabulary. Updates: CLI Short/Long for agentsCmd and the four subcommands, output strings (e.g. 'Agent guidance template version' replaces 'Mainline template version'; 'Agent guidance update' replaces 'Mainline agents update'), --theirs flag description, doctor --setup issue messages per state, status suggestion lines per state, and the JSON-visible Reason on update refusal. Also reconciles the on-disk AGENTS.md body with the embedded template (rc8's 'Intent-first workflow' section was lost during the merge ordering between rc8 and the agents-managed-block PR).
summary.why: Reader feedback: `mainline agents check` reads ambiguously — 'agents' sounds like operating on agent identity / activity, but the command operates on instruction files Mainline writes for agents. Naming the surface 'agent guidance' in every help line and output string disambiguates it without renaming the namespace, which has its own value (short, maps to AGENTS.md, leaves room for future agent-facing surfaces like hooks / policy / context injection).
summary.user_goal: Make `mainline agents check` read unambiguously as 'check Mainline's integration surface for coding agents' and not as 'check agent instances'.

decisions.chose: Keep `mainline agents` as the umbrella for all agent-facing integration surfaces.
decisions.rationale: Short, maps to AGENTS.md (the de-facto industry filename), and leaves room for future agent-related commands (hooks, policy, context injection) without another namespace.

decisions.chose: 'Agent guidance' across all help text, output, and JSON Reasons.
decisions.rationale: Names what the artefact IS rather than how Mainline implements it (managed block). Distinguishes from 'agent identity' or 'agent activity' which the namespace alone would not.

decisions.chose: Keep 'managed block' in Go code comments and internal struct names (AgentsManagedBlock, AgentsBlockState, writeManagedBlock).
decisions.rationale: 'Managed block' precisely describes the marker-bounded region — that engineering concept is real and load-bearing. Users just don't need it.

decisions.chose: Rename strings now while user base is small.
decisions.rationale: Cost-of-change is near zero today; same change after wider adoption would force string parsers / docs / muscle memory to migrate. Cheap to do, expensive to defer.

rejected.reason: More accurate label for the artefact, but loses the AGENTS.md mental shortcut and pre-emptively narrows the namespace to instruction files only. `agents` is the better umbrella for future agent-facing surfaces.
Too tied to one specific file shape (Copilot's `copilot-instructions.md`). The namespace will eventually carry more than instruction files.
Real reader feedback that 'managed block' / 'Mainline template' leaked internal vocabulary; users had to learn implementation details to read help.

risks: Docs / READMEs / blog posts mentioning 'managed block' phrasing now drift from the CLI output. Tracked in followups.
followups: Audit README and any external docs for 'managed block' / 'Mainline template version' phrasing and align with the new copy.
If `mainline agents` later grows commands beyond instruction-file management (hooks, policy, context injection), revisit the Long help text to reflect the broader scope.

architectural_claims: User-facing terminology (agent guidance) and internal engineering vocabulary (managed block) are distinct vocabularies.
The `mainline agents` namespace is broader than instruction-file management — it is the umbrella for Mainline's integration surface with coding agents.
behavioral_changes: CLI help and output strings replace 'managed block' / 'Mainline template' with 'agent guidance' across `mainline agents install/check/diff/update`.
`mainline doctor --setup` issue messages replace 'managed block' with 'agent guidance' across all four AgentsBlockState branches.
`mainline status` suggestion lines reword the agent-guidance prompts.
JSON-visible `Reason` field on update refusal: 'agent guidance has local edits; pass --theirs to overwrite' (was 'managed block has local edits...').

api_changes.signature: mainline agents install|check|diff|update help text and output (no behavior change)

data_model_changes.name: AGENTS.md body content
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Reconciles on-disk AGENTS.md with the embedded template — rc8's 'Intent-first workflow' section is now present. No marker version bump; checksum updates to match the new body.

migration_notes: No user action required. Next `mainline agents check` will report in_sync against the new copy.
